title: conostylis aculeata subsp. gracilis
intro: Conostylis aculeata subsp. gracilis, also known as prickly cone-flower or prickly conestylis, is a type of flowering plant in the sedge family (Cyperaceae) that is native to Western Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Conostylis aculeata subsp. gracilis are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu. The plant can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues and skin conditions.
